description_long: This triumphal arch is a simple, semi-circular structure with a smooth, continuous curve forming the archway. The exterior surface appears to be made of a uniform, reddish-brown material, likely resembling terracotta or brick, with no visible decorative elements or sculptural reliefs. The arch is relatively thin in cross-section, with consistent thickness throughout its span. Both ends of the arch terminate in flat, squared bases that would rest on the ground. There are no columns, pilasters, or inscription panels present, giving the structure a minimalist and utilitarian appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of early Roman or utilitarian urban arches, intended primarily as a passageway or marker.
description: A minimalist, reddish-brown semi-circular triumphal arch with a smooth surface and no decorative elements. The arch is thin, with squared bases and a consistent curve.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thin, flat profile of the arch with no visible details.
description_view_1: Top-down view revealing the full semi-circular curve and the open archway beneath.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, identical to view 0, showing the flat profile.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view displaying the underside of the arch and the open passageway.
